Architecture is more than shelter — it is a spatial narrative. My work is rooted in human-centric design, prioritising the threshold between private sanctuary and public life, shaped through light, shadow, and materiality.

The Person

About Me

I am Yatri Ketan Kumar Shah, an architecture student at Parul University, Vadodara, pursuing a Bachelor of Architecture (2023–2028). My academic journey began with a Diploma in Architecture, and I have since developed a practice grounded in contextual, human-centric design.

My projects explore the intersection of culture, climate, and community — from housing for artisan workers in Kutch to cultural institutions rooted in Vadnagar’s heritage. Through two internships, I have translated studio thinking into built reality, gaining experience in working drawings, site execution, and interior design.
